resolvedQuestions.question: Common root cause for doc/schema-to-code drift
resolvedQuestions.answer: Divergent hand-maintained contract surfaces: public tool definitions, runtime Zod schemas, allowed-parameter maps, handlers, docs, catalog, and playbook.

resolvedQuestions.question: Metadata-drift systemic-ness
resolvedQuestions.answer: Systemic enough for tooling. Backfill and canonical save refresh metadata, but current checks do not enforce all semantic status, last-active, renumbering, and placeholder invariants.

resolvedQuestions.question: Memory-correctness real impact
resolvedQuestions.answer: Entity-density staleness affects graph-channel routing until invalidation/TTL; atomic save ordering can create an indexed row before final file promotion.

resolvedQuestions.question: P0 security severity calibration
resolvedQuestions.answer: Community fallback is P0 if governed scope is a data boundary; causal bare-ID mutation is P1 under strictly local single-user operation and P0 if tenant/user/agent scope is security isolation.

resolvedQuestions.question: Deep-loop blast radius
resolvedQuestions.answer: Prior fan-out orchestration summaries can mask failed CLI exits and under-delivered concurrency. The eight slice summaries in this audit report one CLI lineage despite five report files per slice.

keyFindings.title: MCP tool contracts are split across multiple maintained sources
keyFindings.impact: Docs and tool schemas drift because no single generated contract forces parity.

keyFindings.title: Graph metadata and launch metadata require semantic freshness checks
keyFindings.impact: Resume, launch, and completion routing can trust stale pointers or placeholder-complete children.

keyFindings.title: Memory write path has real stale-routing and DB/file-ordering failure modes
keyFindings.impact: Normal operator actions can cause temporary retrieval/routing drift or indexed records pointing at non-promoted files.

keyFindings.title: Governed-scope bypasses are real, but severity depends on the declared boundary
keyFindings.impact: Community fallback can leak out-of-scope rows; causal ID tools can mutate or delete out-of-scope edges if scope is a security boundary.

keyFindings.title: Fan-out orchestration can count failed child exits as success
keyFindings.impact: Prior review/research campaign summaries may understate failed lineages and overstate concurrency.

ruledOutDirections.reason: The failure is contract parity across runtime schemas, docs, and tests.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Trust existing metadata backfill as sufficient
ruledOutDirections.reason: The backfill identifies thin/ambiguous metadata but does not prove launch/completion truth.
The summary source can count failed subprocess returns as fulfilled lineage work.
